analysis.reasoning: Core message: A Russian UN representative claims UN Human Rights Council discussions on Hong Kong’s legislation are biased and politicized, rejects external interference in China’s domestic affairs. Evaluation: RU1 (West immoral/hostile): Implicitly portrays Western-led criticism at the UN as biased/politicized, suggesting hostility and unfairness from Western actors. This aligns with depicting the West as acting in bad faith; therefore, promoted. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system): The tweet defends sovereignty and non-interference, themes of multipolarity, but it does not present Russia as leading or showcasing an alternative system or cooperation mechanism; not promoted. RU3 (Russia benevolent partner): No emphasis on Russia helping or partnering constructively; not promoted. RU4 (Russian history admirable): Not relevant. RU5 (Russia at the forefront): Not relevant. RU6 (Russia culture/nature): Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes RU1.
